advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a live/young-lock safety-negative test for the delete and init._git paths (plant a fresh lock that is never released; assert the write fails and the lock is left intact) mirroring test_live_young_index_lock_is_not_reclaimed.
evidence: test_delete_rides_out_contended_index_lock asserts only `rc == 0` and `.tombstone.json` presence (lines 106-107); test_init_git_rides_out_contended_index_lock asserts only `cp.returncode == 0` (line 119)

Reference suite has a dedicated safety-negative: tests/interfaces/store/test_event_append_index_lock.py:102 `def test_live_young_index_lock_is_not_reclaimed` — asserting `lock.exists()` after the write (line 111)

gitutil._reclaim_if_stale_index_lock removes the lock only when `age > _INDEX_LOCK_STALE_S` (src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py:103); a regression that dropped this age guard would still leave the two new contended tests green, since a force-removed fresh lock also yields a successful write

ABSENCE: no `test_live_young_index_lock` / `not_reclaimed` test exists under tests/interfaces/lifecycle for the delete or init paths (search for `not_reclaimed` in tests/interfaces/lifecycle returned no matches)
advisory.finding: The two new contended-ride-out tests assert only final success and cannot distinguish 'rode out via retry' from 'wrongly force-removed a fresh (live-peer) lock' — the delete/init suite lacks the safety-negative test (a young lock never released must NOT be reclaimed and the write must fail) that event_append pins via test_live_young_index_lock_is_not_reclaimed, leaving the index-corruption regression on these paths uncovered.
